title: Pseudo-horizon multitask stacker
summary: Recent F1 pit-stop work reports gains from predicting both next-lap pit probability and remaining laps until the next stop; the auxiliary horizon target should expose structure that hard pit-window heuristics miss.
rationale: Recent F1 pit-stop work reports gains from predicting both next-lap pit probability and remaining laps until the next stop; the auxiliary horizon target should expose structure that hard pit-window heuristics miss.
implementation_hint: Within each Year/Race/Driver sequence, derive `laps_to_next_stop` from the next observed `PitStop==1` in train only, cap it (for example 12-15), train a regression/ordinal model on that target, then feed its OOF prediction into the final binary classifier as a meta-feature.
expected_effect: Better separation of borderline cases where the car is not pitting now but is clearly approaching the stop window.
risk: Pseudo-label construction must be strictly fold-safe and the regression target will be noisy near race end or incomplete stints.
